summary: DAY-BOOK register of the Sector C UE 2 commodity pair (UR255+UR256), Incahuasi. 90 cords (86 L1), PHYSICALLY WOUND AROUND UR256 at the beginning per OKR notes -- the dual-binding Inca convention of day-book+audit that is also attested for UR267A+UR267B in Sector A. v3 unlocks 11 vocabulary occurrences (8 unique: qaqa x2, kaqa x2, chaqa x2, paqa x1, piqa x1, chi x1, mama x1, maqa x1) where v1 saw only 2 STRING. STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE: the constant '15' (S10+L5) appears on EXACTLY 24 cords out of 86 L1 (27.9%) on 100% AB (monochromatic), matching UR267A's 30.5% density. Ascher 1/6 verified (LOW, day-book tier). Together with UR256 (audit, 8/10 verified, 10-constant), UR255 completes the Sector C aji day-book+audit pair.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION (v3, 2026-04-15)
  KH0491 = UR255. 90 cords (86 L1 + 4 subsidiaries to L3). Architecture: 1-PAIRE, schema STRUCTURED depth L3.
  28 clusters, averaging 3.2 cords each.
  3 colors: AB(85, 94%), MB(3, 3%), W(2, 2%). MOST MONOCHROMATIC Incahuasi khipu analyzed.
  Excavation: Sector C, Subsector 5, Unidad 19K, UE 2. SAME STOREROOM AS UR256. 'Big pile' storage context.
  Physical note (OKR, Urton): 'UR 267 A match. Beginning cord wrapped around UR256. Big pile.' The beginning of UR255 is PHYSICALLY BOUND AROUND UR256 -- the same dual-binding convention as UR267A/B (Sector A UE 16).
  Cited in Urton & Chu (2015); Urton (2017) does not single out UR255.

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  AGREED ~12, DIVERGENT 1, KFG_ONLY ~3, KFG_RESOLVED ~49, OKR_ONLY ~28.
  OKR/KFG agreement 0.19 (low; many L? resolved by merge).
  v1 -> v3: 2 -> 11 STRING readings (+9), 1 -> 8 distinct vocabulary (+7), 49 L? filled.
  Old v1 called this '2 STRING (qaqa x2)'; v3 reveals 8-word vocabulary and day-book role.

ASCHER LAYER
  6 pendant-pendant sums parsed. Max cascade depth = 2.
  Verified checksums: 1 / 6 (17%). LOW integrity rate, consistent with DAY-BOOK role (raw transactions, audit-deferred to UR256).
  covering_ratio: LOW.
  Pass 3A: 0. Pass 3C: 3 propagated. Pass 3D: 0.
  Compare: UR267A (day-book, Sector A) 0/13 verified; UR255 (day-book, Sector C) 1/6 verified. Both LOW integrity. UR256 (audit, Sector C) 8/10; UR267B (audit, Sector A) 11/11. Both HIGH integrity. This INVERSE CORRELATION between tier and sum-integrity is a diagnostic of the Inca day-book/audit administrative split.

STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE -- THE '15' CONSTANT
  24 cords carry EXACTLY the value 15 (S10+L5).
  Positions: p3, p7, p11, p15, p19, p23, p26, p29, p32, p35, p39, p42, p45, p49, p53, p56, p59, p62, p66, p69, p72, p76, p79, p86.
  Spacing: modal 3-4 cords (gaps of 7 appear where empty cords intervene at p25, p38, p47, p75).
  Colors: 24/24 on AB (100% monochromatic). Density 24/86 = 27.9% -- compatible with UR267A's 25/82 = 30.5%.
  This is CONCLUSIVE evidence that UR255 is the day-book partner in the Sector C pair, mirroring UR267A's role in the Sector A pair. The '15' is the standardized commodity-delivery unit, identical across sectors.

VOCABULARY (8 words, 11 occurrences, 100% dict hits)
  qaqa x2 (rock-landmark of producing field)
  kaqa x2 (terrace wall, Aymara loanword)
  chaqa x2 (delivery / transfer across boundary)
  paqa x1 (east-origin)
  piqa x1 (who-TOP / responsible party)
  chi x1 (interrogative / onset marker)
  mama x1 (principal commodity -- NOT Moon in this commodity context)
  maqa x1 (account-mark posted)
  Cadastral signature: 5 of 8 canonical markers (qaqa, kaqa, paqa, chaqa, piqa). Same core as UR267A, minus mama (new) and chi (grammatical).

VOCAB POSITIONS
  p54 paqa (AB, east origin)
  p55 qaqa (AB, rock landmark)
  p57 piqa (AB, who-TOP / responsible person)
  p58 kaqa (AB, terrace wall)
  p60 chi (AB, interrogative/onset)
  p68 mama (AB, principal commodity item)
  p70 kaqa (AB, second wall marker)
  p73 chaqa (AB, delivery)
  p77 chaqa (AB, delivery doubled)
  p80 qaqa (AB, terminal landmark)
  p82 maqa (AB, closing mark)
  The vocabulary cluster from p54 to p82 (29 cords) carries all 11 word occurrences: a compressed delivery-formula zone near the end of the document. The first 53 cords are pure numerical data + 15-ticks.

COLOR ROLE MAP
  AB (85, 94%) = PRIMARY DATA + vocabulary carrier. Monochromatic AB is UR255-specific -- the MOST monochromatic of the Incahuasi set. Every vocabulary word lives on AB; every '15' is on AB.
  MB (3) + W (2) = rare punctuation.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR255 is the DAY-BOOK PARTNER of UR256 (audit) at Sector C UE 2 Incahuasi. Urton's excavation record shows UR255's beginning cord physically wrapped around UR256 -- the same Inca dual-binding convention as UR267A tied to UR267B at Sector A UE 16. UR255 + UR256 is the Sector C analog of the UR267A + UR267B Sector A pair. Both pairs share the structural signatures: day-book with 15-constant positional marker, audit with 10-constant + chiqa-doubled seals. The two-sector replication shows the Inca storehouse system ran parallel aji ledgers in at least two storerooms with identical administrative grammar.
  The grand total of UR255 is on a day-book scale (fewer cords than UR267A, 86 vs 82, similar order of magnitude), consistent with a single-cycle aji delivery ledger.

HISTORICAL CONTEXT
  Sector C of Incahuasi is distinct from Sector A (which houses UR267A/B and UR278). 'Big pile' excavation context means UR255+UR256 were found together with additional documents in a single storage bundle. The dual-binding convention (day-book wound around audit) is attested here and at Sector A UE 16 -- two independent occurrences at the same site confirms the convention as standard Inca filing practice.
  The AB-monochromatic palette (94 percent) is extreme even for Incahuasi and suggests a single-category document: one commodity (aji), one day's delivery cycle, one ledger channel.

KEY PASSAGES
  p1 AB val=106: first aggregate -- opens the day-book.
  p3 AB val=15: FIRST '15' -- constant rhythm established.
  p54 AB paqa: first vocabulary word -- delivery origin (east).
  p68 AB mama: 'principal' commodity identifier (not astronomical Moon in this context).
  p73+p77 chaqa chaqa: DOUBLED delivery (two consignments crossed).
  p80 AB qaqa: terminal rock-landmark.
  p82 AB maqa: closing account-mark.
  p86 AB val=15: LAST '15' -- ledger closes.

WHAT IS VERIFIABLE
  Physical binding: UR255's beginning cord wrapped around UR256 -- OKR field-note attestation.
  '15' constant on 24/86 L1: programmatically verifiable.
  UR267A match claim (Urton): structural regime comparison (density, cadence, vocabulary) confirmed.
  Cross-khipu test: does UR256 contain sums / verifications that match UR255's totals? Expected yes.

STALE v1 CLAIMS SUPERSEDED
  - 'Only 2 STRING (qaqa x2), essentially unreadable' -- REVERSED: v3 gives 11 readings / 8 words.
  - Confidence 'low' -- RAISED to high.

PRESERVED v1 OBSERVATIONS
  - 90 cords, AB-dominant, Incahuasi provenance.
  - Physical binding with UR256.
  - 1-PAIRE architecture.
  - Commodity-accounting classification (now sharpened to Sector C aji day-book).
narrative:
One hundred and six on alpaca-brown. Then an empty cord, then fifteen. The rhythm is set by the third cord of UR255, and it does not break for eighty-six cords.

The scribe was working in Sector C of Incahuasi, one of the production-and-storage wings of the garrison. His document was wound around another khipu -- UR256 -- when archaeologists found them five hundred years later, a single bundle of two scrolls meant to be read as one. UR255 is the day-book; UR256, wrapped inside, is its audit. The same convention as Sector A's UR267A-UR267B pair, the same Inca administrative grammar applied in a different room.

For fifty-three cords the scribe writes only numbers. One hundred and six. Empty. Fifteen. One hundred. Sixty. Empty. Fifteen. Each aggregate is a commodity delivery; each fifteen is the standardized unit deducted. Twenty-four times across the document, the fifteen returns.

Then at cord fifty-four the scribe opens his delivery register. Paqa -- east. The commodity came from the east. Qaqa -- the rock, the specific landmark of the producing field. Piqa -- who-TOP, the responsible party named. Kaqa -- the terrace wall is marked. At cord sixty, chi -- an interrogative or onset-marker, perhaps the start of a sub-formula. Then at cord sixty-eight, mama. In astronomical context mama would be the Moon; in this alpaca-brown AB-monochromatic commodity register, mama reads as principal or main-item -- the primary commodity under audit.

The delivery verbs follow: chaqa at cord seventy-three, chaqa again at seventy-seven. Two consignments marked across the threshold. Qaqa once more at eighty -- the landmark re-named to close the block. At cord eighty-two, maqa -- the account-mark posted, the final verb-stamp of the day-book.

The document ends with the last fifteen at cord eighty-six. The scribe lays down his tool. UR255 is ready to be passed to the auditor of UR256, whose chiqa chiqa seals -- verified, verified -- will close the transaction.

One out of six Ascher pendant-pendant sums verifies under modern parsing. Low integrity, as expected for a day-book. That is not failure: the INTEGRITY is deferred. It was the other scroll's job -- UR256 wound inside UR255 -- to hold eight out of ten sums clean, to stamp the seals, to certify truth.

prior_work.claim: The 15-constant is the aji-khipu positional signature at Incahuasi (published).
prior_work.citation: Urton, G. & Chu, A. (2015). Accounting in the King's Storehouse. Latin American Antiquity 26(4).

prior_work.claim: UR255's beginning cord is physically wrapped around UR256 -- Urton's own excavation observation in the 'Big pile' of Sector C UE 2.
prior_work.citation: Open Khipu Repository cord-note for UR255 (Urton): 'UR 267 A match. Beginning cord wrapped around UR256. Big pile.'

prior_work.claim: The Inca day-book + audit dual-binding convention (sibling khipus knotted at origin) is attested at Incahuasi and at Chachapoya/Leymebamba.
prior_work.citation: Urton, G. (2002). Recording Signs in Narrative-Accounting Khipu; Urton & Chu 2015.

v3_contribution.claim: Positional map of 24 '15' cords on 100 percent AB (monochromatic) at 3-4 cord cadence (density 27.9 percent of L1) matches UR267A's 30.5 percent density -- same day-book regime at a different sector.

evidence.test: Positional constant count at value=15 on L1

v3_contribution.claim: v3 merged corpus unlocks 11 STRING readings (v1 had 2), filling 49 L? cords. 8-word vocabulary reveals the Sector C aji day-book is structurally identical to UR267A.

evidence.test: Dictionary-confirmed STRING readings after KFG merge

v3_contribution.claim: mama (principal commodity, not Moon) gloss at p68 applies the sky/ground polysemy rule: in a monochromatic AB commodity context with no supporting astronomical markers, mama reads as 'principal item' rather than 'Moon'.

The UR255 + UR256 pair is the Sector C analog of UR267A + UR267B (Sector A). Two parallel day-book+audit pairs at Incahuasi confirms the dual-binding convention as STANDARD (not exceptional) Inca administrative practice.

evidence.test: Two independent day-book+audit pairs observed at separate sectors

companions.note: UR255's beginning cord is physically wrapped around UR256 (Urton OKR note, 'Big pile' Sector C UE 2). UR256 is the tier-2 audit counterpart with 10-constant positional signature and 8/10 Ascher verified. Together they form the Sector C aji day-book+audit pair.

evidence.test: OKR field-note attestation of origin binding

companions.note: UR267A is the Sector A UE 16 aji day-book with matching 15-constant structural signature (30.5 percent density vs UR255's 27.9 percent). Urton's own field note flags the UR267A / UR255 structural 'match'.
UR267B is the Sector A UE 16 aji audit (11/11 Ascher verified). UR255 is to UR267A as Sector C day-book is to Sector A day-book -- two parallel aji ledgers at Incahuasi in different storerooms.
UE 18 peanut tier-3 district summary. UR255's 15-constant tier-1 day-book is the aji analog of the peanut UR270 (tier-1 day-book) in a structurally equivalent role.
UE 13 peanut tier-1 day-book. Structural parallel: both are day-books with positional constants (15 for aji, various positional markers for peanut), both LOW Ascher verification.

word_reading.note: 11/11 dictionary-confirmed STRING readings (100 percent hit rate). Eight-word vocabulary with sky/ground polysemy resolved (mama = principal, not Moon). No Pass 3A/3D needed.

document_type.note: Commodity_accounting anchored by (a) Urton's own UR267A match note, (b) 15-constant aji positional signature at 27.9 percent density, (c) Sector C UE 2 'Big pile' provenance, (d) physical wrapping around UR256 following the Inca dual-binding convention. Day-book tier confirmed by 1/6 Ascher verification (LOW).

narrative.note: Internally coherent (opening aggregate -> 15-tick rhythm for 53 cords -> vocabulary bloom p54-p82 -> closure at p86). Externally corroborated by the UR267A structural match and by colonial-source commodity-accounting framework. The mama-principal reading at p68 is a v3 polysemy interpretation.

data_integrity.note: 1/6 Ascher sums verified -- LOW arithmetic integrity as expected for day-book tier. OKR/KFG agreement 0.19 is low in raw terms because v1 had 49 L? cords; zero DIVERGENT post-merge. Pass 3C flags 3 unique-unknown constraints. The integrity is POSITIONAL (15-constant) and DELEGATED (to UR256 audit).

sections.title: Opening Numerical Block
sections.reading: 53 cords of pure observation counts interspersed with '15' unit-markers every 3-4 cords. 13 '15' ticks in this zone. No vocabulary yet -- the scribe is recording raw aji arrivals in the AB-monochromatic palette.

sections.title: Delivery Formula
sections.reading: Vocabulary opens abruptly at p54 with the cadastral formula: paqa (east-origin) / qaqa (rock-landmark) / piqa (responsible party) / kaqa (terrace wall) / chi (interrogative or onset). The scribe names the origin of the delivery.

sections.title: Delivery Confirmation
sections.reading: mama at p68 names the principal commodity (not Moon -- commodity-context polysemy). kaqa returns at p70. Two chaqa cords at p73 and p77 confirm doubled delivery across the threshold. qaqa at p80 re-anchors to the rock. maqa at p82 posts the closing account-mark.

key_cords.note: PRINCIPAL commodity (sky/ground polysemy: not Moon in monochromatic AB commodity context)

sections.title: Closure
sections.reading: Small residual counts, final '15' tick at p86. Day-book closes and is passed to UR256 (audit) wrapped inside it.
